name: Benin
excerpt: Present-day Benin is comprised of about 42 ethnic groups, including the Yoruba in the southeast, who migrated from what is now Nigeria in the 12th century; the Dendi in the north-central area, who came from Mali in the 16th century; the Bariba and the Fula in the northeast; the…

quick_facts.languages: 55 languages; French (official); Fon (a Gbe language), Yom (a Gur language) and Yoruba are the most important indigenous languages in the south; half a dozen regionally important languages in the north, including Bariba and Fulfulde
quick_facts.religions: Muslim 27.7%, Roman Catholic 25.5%, Protestant 13.5% (Celestial 6.7%, Methodist 3.4%, other Protestant 3.4%), Vodoun 11.6%, other Christian 9.5%, other traditional religions 2.6%, other 2.6%, none 5.8% (2013 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Fon and related 38.4%, Adja and related 15.1%, Yoruba and related 12%, Bariba and related 9.6%, Fulani and related 8.6%, Ottamari and related 6.1%, Yoa-Lokpa and related 4.3%, Dendi and related 2.9%, other 0.9%, foreigner 1.9% (2013 est.)
quick_facts.climate: tropical; hot, humid in south; semiarid in north
quick_facts.terrain: mostly flat to undulating plain; some hills and low mountains
quick_facts.natural_resources: small offshore oil deposits, limestone, marble, timber
quick_facts.major_exports: gold, cotton, coconuts/brazil nuts/cashews, soybeans, wood (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: sandbanks create difficult access to a coast with no natural harbors, river mouths, or islands
summaries.history_summary: Present-day Benin is comprised of about 42 ethnic groups, including the Yoruba in the southeast, who migrated from what is now Nigeria in the 12th century; the Dendi in the north-central area, who came from Mali in the 16th century; the Bariba and the Fula in the northeast; the Ottamari in the Atakora mountains; the Fon in the area around Abomey in the south-central area; and the Mina, Xueda, and Aja, who came from Togo, on the coast. The Kingdom of Dahomey emerged on the Abomey plateau in the 17th century and was a regional power for much of the 18th and 19th centuries. The growth of Dahomey coincided with the growth of the Atlantic slave trade, and it became known as a major source of enslaved people. France began to control the coastal areas of Dahomey in the second half of the 19th century; the entire kingdom was conquered by 1894. French Dahomey achieved independence in 1960, and it changed its name to the Republic of Benin in 1975.A succession of military governments ended in 1972 with the rise to power of Mathieu KEREKOU and a Marxist-Leninist government. A move to representative government began in 1989. Two years later, free elections ushered in former Prime Minister Nicephore SOGLO as president, marking the first successful transfer of power in Africa from a dictatorship to a democracy. KEREKOU returned to power after elections in 1996 and 2001. He stepped down in 2006 and was succeeded by Thomas YAYI Boni, a political outsider and independent, who won a second term in 2011. Patrice TALON, a wealthy businessman, took office in 2016; the space for pluralism, dissent, and free expression has narrowed under his administration. TALON won a second term in 2021.
summaries.economy_summary: robust economic growth; slightly declining but still widespread poverty; strong trade relations with Nigeria; cotton exporter; COVID-19 has led to capital outflows and border closures; WAEMU member with currency pegged to the euro; recent fiscal deficit and debt reductions

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Benin's spiritual landscape is genuinely mixed — significant Muslim and Catholic populations alongside a real Protestant presence and the traditional Vodoun religion, from which the modern practice of voodoo originated. Pray for the clear gospel to break through in a nation shaped by spiritual syncretism.
prayer_points: Pray for Benin's Protestant churches to be strengthened and equipped with sound biblical teaching.
Pray for those practicing Vodoun and syncretistic folk religion to hear and receive the biblical gospel.
Pray for Benin's Muslim and Catholic populations to encounter genuine, personal faith in Christ.
Pray for pastors to be trained in expository, Scripture-centered preaching.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: Benin's mix of Islam, Catholicism, Protestantism, and traditional Vodoun religion makes it a genuinely complex field. Ongoing needs include strengthening existing Protestant churches, training national pastors, and reaching those practicing syncretistic folk religion with the clear biblical gospel.
